content.content:
1500	    // C: L->errfunc = old_errfunc
1501	    state.errfunc = old_errfunc;
1502	    status
1503	}
1504	
1505	// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1506	// Protected parser
1507	// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1508	
1509	/// Parser invocation data passed through `pcall`.
1510	///
1511	/// C: `struct SParser { ZIO *z; Mbuffer buff; Dyndata dyd; const char *mode; const char *name; }`
1512	///
1513	/// PORT NOTE: `const char *mode` and `const char *name` become owned byte vecs
1514	/// so that `SParser` can outlive the original string data without raw pointers.
1515	struct SParser {
1516	    z: ZIO,
1517	    /// LexBuffer from `crate::zio` (Mbuffer in C).
1518	    buff: LexBuffer,
1519	    /// TODO(phase-b): real Dyndata lives in the lua-parse crate.
1520	    dyd: DynDataStub,
1521	    // C: const char *mode — byte slice for chunk mode ("b", "t", or "bt")
1522	    // PORT NOTE: stored as Option<Vec<u8>> to own the bytes; None means no mode restriction.
1523	    mode: Option<Vec<u8>>,
1524	    // C: const char *name — chunk name (source identifier)
1525	    name: Vec<u8>,
1526	}
1527	
1528	/// Checks that the chunk mode permits loading the given kind ("binary" or "text").
1529	///
1530	/// C: `static void checkmode(lua_State *L, const char *mode, const char *x)`
1531	fn check_mode(
1532	    state: &mut LuaState,
1533	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1534	    kind: &[u8],
1535	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1536	    if let Some(mode_bytes) = mode {
1537	        // C: strchr(mode, x[0]) == NULL  — mode doesn't contain the first letter of kind
1538	        let kind_char = kind[0];
1539	        if !mode_bytes.contains(&kind_char) {
1540	            // C: luaO_pushfstring + luaD_throw(L, LUA_ERRSYNTAX)
1541	            // TODO(port): &[u8] display — lossy UTF-8 here is acceptable for mode/kind
1542	            // strings which are always ASCII literals ("binary"/"text" and "bt"/"b"/"t").
1543	            return Err(LuaError::syntax(format_args!(
1544	                "attempt to load a {} chunk (mode is '{}')",
1545	                core::str::from_utf8(kind).unwrap_or("?"),
1546	                core::str::from_utf8(mode_bytes).unwrap_or("?"),
1547	            )));
1548	        }
1549	    }
1550	    Ok(())
1551	}
1552	
1553	/// Parser callback invoked inside `pcall`: reads the first byte to decide
1554	/// binary vs. text, then calls the undumper or parser accordingly.
1555	///
1556	/// C: `static void f_parser(lua_State *L, void *ud)`
1557	fn f_parser(state: &mut LuaState, p: &mut SParser) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1558	    // C: int c = zgetc(p->z)  — read first character
1559	    // zgetc → z.getc()  (macros.tsv)
1560	    let c = p.z.getc();
1561	
1562	    // C: if (c == LUA_SIGNATURE[0])
1563	    // LUA_SIGNATURE → const LUA_SIGNATURE: &[u8] = b"\x1bLua"  (macros.tsv)
1564	    if c == b'\x1b' as i32 {
1565	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "binary")
1566	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1567	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1568	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1569	        let cl = crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?;
1570	        // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues)
1571	        debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1572	        // C: luaF_initupvals(L, cl)
1573	        func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1574	    } else {
1575	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "text")
1576	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"text")?;
1577	        // C: cl = luaY_parser(L, p->z, &p->buff, &p->dyd, p->name, c)
1578	        // TODO(port): parser API not yet finalised; returns a LClosure.
1579	        let cl = parse_stub(state, &mut p.z, &mut p.buff, &mut p.dyd, &p.name, c)?;
1580	        debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1581	        func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1582	    }
1583	
1584	    Ok(())
1585	}
1586	
1587	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1588	///
1589	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1590	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1591	    state: &mut LuaState,
1592	    z: ZIO,
1593	    name: &[u8],
1594	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1595	) -> LuaStatus {
1596	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1597	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1598	    state.inc_nny();
1599	
1600	    let mut p = SParser {
1601	        z,
1602	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1603	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1604	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1605	        name: name.to_vec(),
1606	    };
1607	
1608	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1609	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1610	
1611	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1612	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1613	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1614	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1615	
1616	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1617	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1618	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1619

1619	
1620	    // C: decnny(L)
1621	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1622	    state.dec_nny();
1623	
1624	    status
1625	}
1626	
1627	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1628	// PORT STATUS
1629	//   source:        src/ldo.c  (1029 lines, ~37 functions translated, 2 omitted)
1630	//   target_crate:  lua-vm
1631	//   confidence:    medium
1632	//   todos:         23
1633	//   port_notes:    13
1634	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
1635	//   notes:         Core call/stack/error machinery translated faithfully.
1636	//                  setjmp/longjmp → Result<T,LuaError> throughout.
1637	//                  relstack/correctstack omitted (StackIdx already offset-based).
1638	//                  Coroutine functions (lua_resume, lua_yieldk, resume, unroll,
1639	//                  etc.) are translated but require Phase E stack-switching to
1640	//                  actually work.  Hook-callback borrow conflict flagged as
1641	//                  TODO(port) in hook() and finish_ccall(); Phase E must solve.
1642	//                  All method calls (check_stack, gc_check_step, get_ci*,
1643	//                  set_ci*, next_ci, etc.) are best-guess stubs to be wired
1644	//                  up in Phase B once the LuaState API is finalised.
1645	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1646

content.text:
Build is green.

## Report

**Modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1241-1243`

**What it does:** `LuaState::protected_parser` now delegates to the already-implemented `crate::do_::protected_parser(self, z, name, mode)`. The do_.rs version is a faithful port of `luaD_protectedparser` from `ldo.c:1009` — it sets `nny`, constructs an `SParser` (ZIO + LexBuffer + DynDataStub + mode/name), invokes `pcall` over `f_parser`, decrements `nny`, and returns status. The state.rs method was just a missing wrapper, matching the established pattern at line 1244-1246 (`call_no_yield`).

**Next todo!()s likely to surface (downstream of f_parser):**
- `parse_stub` (crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:39) — `todo!("phase-b: hook lua-parse::parse")` will fire for text chunks. Most likely the next panic.
- `pcall` internals if pcall itself hits an unstubbed path.

**cargo build -p lua-cli:** green (warnings only).
